_source: Fundamentals of Nursing, Med-Surg Nursing Textbooks, Hospital Protocols, NCLEX Review
_note: Long-tail nursing knowledge. Assessment findings, interventions, patient safety, lab value interpretation.

heart_rate.elderly: May be lower baseline; assess individual

oxygen_saturation.concerning: <90%; administer O2 and assess respiratory status
oxygen_saturation.chronic_copd: 88-92% may be acceptable baseline; verify with provider

orthostatic_vital_signs.procedure: Measure BP and HR lying, sitting, standing (1-3 min each position)
orthostatic_vital_signs.positive_finding: SBP drop ≥20 mmHg or DBP drop ≥10 mmHg, or HR increase ≥20 bpm
orthostatic_vital_signs.indication: Fall risk, dehydration, hypovolemia, medication side effect
orthostatic_vital_signs.nursing_action: Fall precautions, notify provider, encourage fluids if appropriate

pain_assessment.fifth_vital_sign: Always assess pain; document location, quality, intensity, aggravating/alleviating factors

scales.faces: For children or non-verbal; 6-10 face scale
scales.flacc: Face, Legs, Activity, Cry, Consolability; for infants/non-verbal (0-10 score)

pain_assessment.reassessment: Within 30-60 min after intervention; document effectiveness

five_rights.right_patient: Check two identifiers (name + DOB or MRN); never use room number
five_rights.right_medication: Verify order against MAR; check drug name, dose, route
five_rights.right_dose: Calculate and verify; double-check high-alert meds with second nurse
five_rights.right_route: PO, IV, IM, SQ, topical, etc.; verify route is appropriate
five_rights.right_time: Within 30-60 min of scheduled time (institutional policy)

three_checks: Check label when removing from dispensing unit
Check label before administering to patient

iv_medication_safety.verify_patency: Assess IV site before administration; flush with saline; check for infiltration/phlebitis
iv_medication_safety.rate_calculation: mL/hr = (total volume × drop factor) / time in minutes
iv_medication_safety.example: Infuse 100 mL over 30 min with 20 gtt/mL tubing: (100×20)/30 = 67 gtt/min
iv_medication_safety.smart_pump: Use when available; reduces calculation errors; still verify settings

types_and_onset.rapid_acting: Lispro, Aspart; onset 15 min; peak 1 hr; give with meals
types_and_onset.short_acting: Regular; onset 30 min; peak 2-3 hr; give 30 min before meals
types_and_onset.intermediate: NPH; onset 2-4 hr; peak 4-10 hr; cloudy; roll, don't shake
types_and_onset.long_acting: Glargine, Detemir; onset 2-4 hr; no peak; basal coverage

insulin_administration.mixing_insulin: Draw up clear (regular) before cloudy (NPH); 'clear before cloudy'
insulin_administration.rotation_sites: Abdomen (fastest absorption), arms, thighs, buttocks; rotate within same region
insulin_administration.never_abbreviate: Write 'units' not 'U'; 10U can be read as 100; fatal error

heparin.frequency: Check 6 hr after rate change or bolus, then daily when stable
heparin.reversal: Protamine sulfate 1 mg per 100 units heparin (given in last 2-3 hr)

warfarin.lab: INR; goal 2-3 for most (DVT, AFib); 2.5-3.5 for mechanical valve
warfarin.frequency: Daily until stable, then weekly to monthly
warfarin.reversal: Vitamin K 2.5-10 mg (onset 6-24 hr); FFP or PCC for immediate reversal
warfarin.teaching: Consistent vitamin K intake; avoid cranberry juice; report bleeding

hemoglobin.low: Anemia; assess for bleeding, fatigue, pallor, tachycardia
hemoglobin.critical_low: <7 g/dL; may need transfusion; notify provider immediately

hematocrit.relationship: Hct approximately 3× Hgb; both decrease with bleeding/anemia

platelets.precautions: Avoid IM injections, rectal temps, NSAIDs; bleeding precautions; platelet transfusion if <10,000 or bleeding

wbc.neutropenia: Absolute neutrophil count <1,500; infection risk; <500 is severe (neutropenic precautions)

sodium.hyponatremia: <135; confusion, seizures if severe (<120); causes: SIADH, diuretics, fluid overload
sodium.hypernatremia: >145; confusion, seizures; causes: dehydration, diabetes insipidus, excessive sodium intake

potassium.hypokalemia: <3.5; dysrhythmias, muscle weakness, ileus; EKG: flat T waves, U waves, ST depression
potassium.hyperkalemia: >5.0; life-threatening dysrhythmias; EKG: peaked T waves, wide QRS; >6.5 is critical
potassium.critical_high: >6.0; notify provider stat; may need calcium gluconate, insulin + D50, kayexalate, dialysis

glucose.hypoglycemia: <70; give 15g carbs (4 oz juice, 3-4 glucose tabs); recheck in 15 min
glucose.severe_hypoglycemia: <50 or altered LOC; give D50 1 amp IV or glucagon 1 mg IM
glucose.hyperglycemia: >200 fasting; assess for DKA (Kussmaul breathing, fruity breath, ketones)

creatinine.elevated: Kidney dysfunction; hold nephrotoxic drugs (NSAIDs, aminoglycosides); assess urine output

bun.elevated: Dehydration, renal failure, GI bleed, high protein diet
bun.bun_cr_ratio: >20:1 suggests dehydration; <10:1 suggests liver disease or malnutrition

interpretation.acidosis: pH <7.35; respiratory if PaCO2 >45; metabolic if HCO3 <22
interpretation.alkalosis: pH >7.45; respiratory if PaCO2 <35; metabolic if HCO3 >26
interpretation.compensation: Opposite component moves to normalize pH; partial if pH still abnormal, full if pH normal

examples.copd_exacerbation: pH 7.32, PaCO2 58, HCO3 28; respiratory acidosis with partial metabolic compensation
examples.diabetic_ketoacidosis: pH 7.25, PaCO2 28, HCO3 14; metabolic acidosis with partial respiratory compensation

fall_prevention.risk_factors: Age >65, history of falls, orthostatic hypotension, altered LOC, medications (sedatives, antihypertensives)
fall_prevention.morse_fall_scale: Score ≥45 = high risk; assess history of falls, secondary diagnosis, ambulatory aid, IV, gait, mental status
interventions: Bed in low position, locked
Call bell within reach
Hourly rounding ('I'm here to help you with pain, potty, position, proximity')
Non-skid footwear
Adequate lighting
Clear path to bathroom
Bedside commode if indicated
fall_prevention.restraints_last_resort: Try all alternatives first; require provider order; document every 2 hr; release q2h; never PRN order

pressure_injury_prevention.braden_scale: Score ≤18 = at risk; assess sensory perception, moisture, activity, mobility, nutrition, friction/shear

interventions.repositioning: Turn q2h minimum; 30° lateral positioning preferred over 90°
interventions.support_surfaces: Pressure-redistributing mattress if Braden ≤18; specialty bed if multiple large ulcers
interventions.skin_care: Keep clean and dry; moisturize; avoid massage over bony prominences (can damage tissue)
interventions.nutrition: Adequate protein (1.25-1.5 g/kg); vitamin C, zinc for healing; consult dietitian

staging.stage_2: Partial thickness; shallow pink wound or intact/ruptured blister
staging.stage_3: Full thickness; visible fat; no bone/tendon visible
staging.stage_4: Full thickness; bone, tendon, or muscle exposed
staging.unstageable: Full thickness; obscured by eschar or slough
staging.deep_tissue_injury: Purple/maroon intact skin or blood-filled blister; suspect deep damage

infection_control.hand_hygiene: Before patient contact, before aseptic task, after body fluid exposure, after patient contact, after contact with patient surroundings

transmission_precautions.standard: All patients; hand hygiene, PPE for body fluid contact
transmission_precautions.contact: C. diff, MRSA, VRE, scabies; gown + gloves; dedicated equipment
transmission_precautions.droplet: Influenza, pertussis, meningococcus; surgical mask within 3 feet
transmission_precautions.airborne: TB, measles, varicella; N95 respirator; negative pressure room

central_line_bundle.insertion: Maximal sterile barrier, chlorhexidine prep, avoid femoral site, daily assessment of need
central_line_bundle.maintenance: Hand hygiene, sterile dressing changes, scrub hub 15 sec before access, assess daily for removal

acute_pain.assessment: Location, quality, intensity (0-10), duration, aggravating/alleviating factors
interventions: Administer analgesics as ordered; reassess 30-60 min post-administration
Non-pharmacologic: repositioning, heat/cold, distraction, relaxation techniques
Assess for adverse effects (sedation, respiratory depression, constipation)
Patient education on pain scale and when to request medication

impaired_gas_exchange.assessment: Respiratory rate, depth, effort; SpO2; breath sounds; ABG if ordered; skin color
interventions: Position: High Fowler's or orthopneic to maximize lung expansion
Administer oxygen as ordered; monitor SpO2 continuously
Encourage deep breathing, coughing, incentive spirometry q2h while awake
Assess for secretions; suction if unable to clear independently
Monitor ABG results; notify provider of abnormal values

risk_for_infection.high_risk_populations: Neutropenic, post-op, invasive devices, immunosuppressed, diabetes
interventions: Monitor temperature q4h; report >38°C (100.4°F)
Assess WBC; report abnormal values
Aseptic technique for all invasive procedures and dressing changes
Assess invasive sites for signs of infection (redness, warmth, drainage, pain)
Encourage adequate nutrition and hydration to support immune function
